Project.name: video2pptx
Project.annotation: Automatically extract slide intervals from educational videos, link to SRT/VTT subtitles, enhance with LLM vision analysis, export presentations, manage projects via GUI/CLI, with video player, subtitle overlay, interactive timeline, and smart manual marker snapping.
Project.keywords: video, slides, markdown, presentation, open-cv, computer-vision, slide-detection, subtitle-alignment, srt, vtt, marp, llm, lm-studio, vision, gui, pyside6, project, timeline, video-player, subtitle-overlay, smart-snap

Actors.actor-User: Content creator with educational videos and optional SRT/VTT subtitles

UC-001.Action: Runs video2pptx detect with video + subtitles (all-in-one)
UC-001.Goal: Get slides.json with slide intervals, representative images, aligned transcript, and optional exports
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: slides.json passes validation, all slides have images, transcript aligned, optional deck.md/deck.pptx generated

UC-002.Goal: Get deck.md — a Marp-formatted Markdown presentation
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: deck.md renders correctly in Marp-compatible viewer

UC-003.Goal: Get debug artifacts: diff_scores.csv, timeline.png, contact_sheet.jpg
UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: Artifacts exist and contain correct data, timeline shows slide changes

UC-004.Action: Runs video2pptx llm-process with slides.json + slides images
UC-004.Goal: Get enriched slides.json with LLM-generated slide descriptions and corrected transcript
UC-004.Preconditions: slides.json exists with slide images, LM Studio running with vision-capable model
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: Each slide has llm_description field with accurate content summary, transcript corrected with slide context terms

UC-005.Action: Runs video2pptx detect-slides with video only (no subtitles)
UC-005.Goal: Get slides.json with slide intervals and representative screenshots — no transcript
UC-005.AcceptanceCriteria: slides.json with slide intervals and images present. No transcript fields. No errors about missing subtitles.

UC-006.Action: Runs video2pptx notes with slides.json + subtitles
UC-006.Goal: Get enhanced slides.json with context-aware notes — combines visual slide analysis with subtitle text
UC-006.Preconditions: slides.json exists with slide images, subtitles or transcript available
UC-006.AcceptanceCriteria: Notes on each slide reflect both what was said AND what is on the slide. Notes are clean, not raw SRT fragments. Can run multiple times with different subtitles/notes-modes without re-detecting slides.

UC-007.Action: Runs video2pptx roi-tool with a video file
UC-007.Goal: Get ignore-roi coordinates (x1,y1,x2,y2) by visually selecting a region on the video frame
UC-007.AcceptanceCriteria: Opens a window showing a frame from the video. User drags a rectangle. Coordinates printed to stdout. Pipes directly into --ignore-roi flag.

UC-008.Action: Creates a new project via CLI or GUI
UC-008.Goal: Get a project.json file that ties together video, subtitles, configs, and pipeline state in one folder
UC-008.AcceptanceCriteria: project.json is created in the specified output folder. All paths are stored as absolute or project-relative. Project can be reopened later.

UC-009.Action: Opens an existing project and runs slide detection via GUI
UC-009.Goal: Slides are detected without blocking the UI; progress is visible; project state is updated
UC-009.Preconditions: Project exists with valid video path; video file exists
UC-009.AcceptanceCriteria: UI shows progress during detection. After completion, timeline appears with slide intervals. project.json state updated to detect_done=true. project.json slides_json path points to valid output.

UC-010.Action: Views detected slides on a timeline and inspects subtitle text per interval
UC-010.Goal: See entire video as a visual timeline with slide segments; click a segment to see its subtitle text
UC-010.Preconditions: Detection completed, slides.json exists with intervals and images
UC-010.AcceptanceCriteria: Timeline shows colored blocks for each slide interval. Clicking a block displays subtitle text for that segment in a side panel. Timecodes are shown.

UC-011.Action: Opens Settings dialog and edits LLM system prompt
UC-011.Goal: View and modify the SYSTEM_PROMPT_REPHRASE that controls how subtitle text is processed by LLM
UC-011.AcceptanceCriteria: LLM tab shows the current system prompt in an editable text field. Changes are saved to project config and take effect on next LLM processing. Changes persist across app restarts.

UC-012.Goal: Choose which video decoder backend to use (auto detect, force OpenCV CPU, force PyAV CUDA)
UC-012.AcceptanceCriteria: Available backends are displayed (detected at startup). User selects one. Selection is saved to project config and used for next detect run. Info about CUDA availability is shown.

UC-013.Action: Uses menu bar: File > New/Open/Close/Save Project, Import Video, Import Subtitles, Exit
UC-013.Goal: Manage project lifecycle and video/subtitle sources via standard desktop menu
UC-013.AcceptanceCriteria: File menu contains all listed items. Keyboard shortcuts match platform conventions (Ctrl+N, Ctrl+O, Ctrl+S, Ctrl+Q). Close unloads project and resets UI. Import Video/Subtitles replaces sources in current project. Save writes project.json immediately.

UC-014.Action: Opens Project Settings and App Settings from Edit menu
UC-014.Goal: Configure per-project detection parameters (ROI, thresholds) and app-wide defaults (LLM, GPU, snap_mode)
UC-014.Preconditions: GUI is running. Project loaded for Project Settings.
UC-014.AcceptanceCriteria: Project Settings: ROI, ignore_rois, threshold, sample_fps, min_slide_duration, dedupe, min_stable_duration. Saved to project.json. App Settings: LLM model/base_url/context, GPU backend, snap_mode, snap_flat_threshold, default paths. Saved to app-config.yaml. New projects inherit app defaults.

UC-015.Action: Plays video with subtitle overlay in GUI, controls playback and volume
UC-015.Goal: Watch video with synced subtitles, seek to specific timestamps, adjust volume
UC-015.Preconditions: Video file exists and is accessible. Project loaded. Subtitles present (optional).
UC-015.AcceptanceCriteria: Play/pause/stop buttons work. Time counter shows current/total. Seek slider seeks correctly. Volume slider adjusts audio level. Subtitles appear over video and sync with playback position. If no subtitles, video plays without overlay.

UC-016.Action: Views green detected markers on timeline, clicks to open slide image. Adds blue manual markers, which auto-snap to nearest scene boundary
UC-016.Goal: Navigate detected slides, inspect images, place manual markers that snap to scene changes
UC-016.Preconditions: Detection completed (green markers) or not (blue markers only). Slide images exist.
UC-016.AcceptanceCriteria: Green markers appear after detection. Double-click opens slide image in system viewer. Right-click adds blue marker. Blue marker snaps to nearest scene change according to snap_mode (diff_only / fallback_analyze / hybrid). Snapped position reflected on timeline.

UC-017.Action: Chooses analysis quality in Project Settings (fast / detailed experimental / native / custom)
UC-017.Goal: Control Pass1 analysis frame max side without reducing screenshot or PPTX resolution
UC-017.Preconditions: Project open. Dual-resolution detect path available (Phase 19).
UC-017.AcceptanceCriteria: New projects default to fast (analysis_max_side=480). Legacy projects missing the field load as native (None). UI shows «Качество анализа» without 480p/720p labels. Notice states only slide-search is affected; screenshots/PPTX stay full-res. Changing quality after Detect requires confirmation and marks detect+downstream stale. No-op save does not rewrite revision unnecessarily. Detect/Auto use project.detection.analysis_max_side; CLI override still wins when explicit.

NonGoals.item-1: No automatic speech recognition (ASR) — subtitles already prepared
NonGoals.item-4: No web UI with user management or multi-user
NonGoals.item-5: No automatic video download from YouTube/Zoom/Meet
NonGoals.item-6: No real-time video streaming from network/URL — local files only
NonGoals.item-7: No real-time slide detection — only batch processing

Constraints.constraint-1: Must process video without loading entire file into memory (streaming)
Constraints.constraint-2: Must support Windows, Linux, macOS paths cross-platform
Constraints.constraint-3: Must support Cyrillic in paths and subtitle text
Constraints.constraint-4: Must not hard-depend on CUDA — CPU fallback required
Constraints.constraint-5: Slide detection is deterministic CV — no LLM for change detection
Constraints.constraint-6: Core processing must not be coupled to UI — CLI works without GUI deps
Constraints.constraint-7: LLM processing is optional — pipeline works without it
Constraints.constraint-8: LLM client must support OpenAI-compatible API (LM Studio)
Constraints.constraint-9: Model must be unloaded after processing to free VRAM
Constraints.constraint-10: Stage-based pipeline: detect-slides and notes must be independent commands, not one monolithic step
Constraints.constraint-11: ROI tool must use only built-in OpenCV (no PyQt/Tkinter/Gradio)
Constraints.constraint-12: Adding subtitles or changing notes-mode must not require re-running slide detection
Constraints.constraint-13: GUI must be installable as optional extras — `pip install video2pptx[gui]`
Constraints.constraint-14: GUI must not block during long operations — workers required
Constraints.constraint-15: Project JSON must be human-readable and hand-editable

Risks.risk-1: Low-quality or highly compressed video may cause false positives in slide detection
Risks.risk-2: Cyrillic subtitle encoding issues on some platforms
Risks.risk-3: GPU backend may not be available on all systems
Risks.risk-4: Animations and transitions between slides may cause split or missed segments
Risks.risk-5: Webcam overlay and UI elements may cause false slide splits
Risks.risk-6: LM Studio may not be running or model may be unavailable
Risks.risk-7: Vision LLM may hallucinate slide content details
Risks.risk-8: Context window may be exceeded for long slide transcripts
Risks.risk-9: PySide6 adds ~50MB dependency — may conflict with existing Qt installations
Risks.risk-10: QThread lifecycle bugs may cause crashes on project/worker switching
Risks.risk-11: QMediaPlayer may not support all video codecs on all platforms — fallback to frame-by-frame display needed
Risks.risk-12: Subtitle overlay may flicker or desync with rapid seeking
Risks.risk-13: Smart snap may snap to wrong boundary if diff_scores are noisy or window is too small

OpenQuestions.question-1: Should representative timestamp be configurable or fixed at 80% into segment?
OpenQuestions.question-2: Should multiple ignore_rois be specified as repeated --ignore-roi flags or comma-separated?
OpenQuestions.question-3: Should GUI support side-by-side comparison of original vs LLM-processed notes?
OpenQuestions.question-5: Should smart snap window be configurable (seconds before/after marker)?
OpenQuestions.question-6: Should subtitle overlay font size/color be configurable in App Settings?
